features.title: Bank feed reconciliation
features.description: Automatically imports and categorizes bank and credit card transactions for faster reconciliation.

features.title: Time tracking
features.description: Track billable and payroll hours directly within QuickBooks Online, available from the Essentials tier up.

features.title: Inventory and project tracking
features.description: Monitor inventory levels and track project profitability, available starting at the Plus tier.

features.title: Class and location tracking
features.description: Segment income and expenses by department, location, or business unit for more granular reporting.

features.title: Advanced custom reporting
features.description: Build tailored financial reports and dashboards, available at the Advanced tier for larger organizations.

features.title: Third-party app marketplace integration
features.description: Connects with a wide ecosystem of payroll, payments, CRM, and e-commerce apps within the Intuit platform.

pros: Accessible from any browser or device with no software installation required
Automatic updates mean features and compliance changes roll out continuously
Tiered pricing scales cleanly from freelancers up to larger small businesses
Deep integration with QuickBooks Payroll, Payments, and third-party apps
Widely supported by accountants and bookkeepers familiar with the platform
Real-time collaboration with employees and outside accountants without file sharing
cons: Prices have risen significantly year over year, with recent increases of 12 to 17 percent annually
Essentials, Plus, and Advanced tiers cost meaningfully more than entry-level competitors
User limits on lower tiers can force upgrades sooner than expected for growing teams
Some advanced reporting and control features are reserved for the top Advanced tier
Ongoing subscription cost over time can exceed what a one-time desktop license would have cost
Introductory discounted pricing expires after a limited number of months

faqs.question: How much does QuickBooks Online cost
faqs.answer: QuickBooks Online starts at 20 US dollars per month for the Solopreneur tier, with core plans running 38 US dollars per month for Simple Start, 75 US dollars for Essentials, 115 US dollars for Plus, and 275 US dollars per month for Advanced.

faqs.question: What is the difference between Simple Start, Essentials, Plus, and Advanced
faqs.answer: Simple Start covers single-user core accounting; Essentials adds time tracking and up to three users; Plus adds inventory, project tracking, and up to five users; Advanced adds higher user limits, advanced reporting, and stronger controls for larger businesses.

faqs.question: When did QuickBooks Online launch
faqs.answer: Intuit first launched a cloud version of QuickBooks in 2001, though the product was substantially rebuilt in 2013, which is generally considered the turning point for QuickBooks Online's modern functionality.

faqs.question: Does QuickBooks Online raise prices every year
faqs.answer: Yes, QuickBooks Online has typically raised prices about once a year, usually in the summer, with recent annual increases in the range of 12 to 17 percent since 2023.

faqs.question: Is there a free trial for QuickBooks Online
faqs.answer: Intuit typically offers new subscribers either a free trial period or a discounted introductory rate for the first several months before standard pricing applies; specific current offers should be confirmed on the QuickBooks Online pricing page.

faqs.question: How many users can use QuickBooks Online
faqs.answer: User limits vary by tier: Simple Start supports one user, Essentials supports up to three, Plus supports up to five, and Advanced supports a higher number of users suited to larger businesses.

faqs.question: Is QuickBooks Online the same as QuickBooks Desktop
faqs.answer: No. QuickBooks Online is a separate cloud-based, browser-accessible subscription product with its own pricing tiers, distinct from the locally installed QuickBooks Desktop product line.

use_cases.title: Freelancers and sole proprietors
use_cases.description: Independent freelancers use the Solopreneur tier to track income, expenses, and estimated taxes without needing full small business accounting features.
use_cases.audience: Freelancers, gig workers, and sole proprietors

use_cases.title: Small business day-to-day bookkeeping
use_cases.description: Small businesses use Simple Start or Essentials for invoicing, expense tracking, and bank reconciliation from any device.
use_cases.audience: Small business owners and bookkeepers

use_cases.title: Growing businesses with inventory and multiple users
use_cases.description: Businesses managing inventory, projects, and multiple team members use the Plus or Advanced tiers for deeper tracking and reporting.
use_cases.audience: Growing small and midsize businesses with multiple employees

enrichment.short_description: Intuit's employee time tracking and workforce scheduling product, formerly known as TSheets, offering GPS-enabled mobile time tracking, scheduling, and project time reporting that integrates with QuickBooks Online and QuickBooks Payroll.
enrichment.pricing_model: Monthly subscription with a flat base fee plus a per-active-user monthly fee, offered in two tiers (Premium and Elite); a QuickBooks Online subscription is required to use it.
enrichment.starting_price: From 20 US dollars per month base fee plus 10 US dollars per user per month (Premium plan, current 2026 pricing)
enrichment.company_size: Approximately 18,200 employees (Intuit); originally an independent company before its 2018 acquisition
enrichment.headquarters: Mountain View, California, USA (Intuit headquarters); product originated in Boise/Eagle, Idaho as TSheets
enrichment.seo_title: QuickBooks Time Pricing 2026: Premium vs Elite Plans Explained
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Understand QuickBooks Time's current pricing, Premium versus Elite features, and how the former TSheets product fits with QuickBooks Online.
enrichment.llm_readable_summary: QuickBooks Time is Intuit's dedicated employee time tracking and scheduling product, previously known as TSheets before its rebrand. TSheets was founded in 2006 in Eagle, Idaho by CEO Matt Rissell after he identified a gap in the employee time tracking and reporting market, and it grew into a substantial independent time-tracking software company before Intuit announced its acquisition on December 5, 2017, in a deal valued at approximately 340 million US dollars in cash and other consideration; the acquisition closed on January 11, 2018. Following the acquisition, the Eagle/Boise-based company was integrated into Intuit, its offices rebranded as an Intuit site, and the product was gradually renamed from TSheets by QuickBooks to its current name, QuickBooks Time. As a product line within Intuit, which is headquartered in Mountain View, California, and employs roughly 18,200 people globally, QuickBooks Time does not have separate public company financials, though it continues to power time tracking for a large share of QuickBooks Online's small business customer base. QuickBooks Time offers GPS-enabled mobile time tracking so employees can clock in and out from job sites, along with scheduling tools for creating and sharing employee schedules, geofencing to prompt clock-in and clock-out reminders based on location, overtime alerts, and reporting that feeds directly into QuickBooks Online and QuickBooks Payroll for streamlined payroll processing. The product is sold in two tiers: Premium, aimed at companies primarily focused on tracking attendance and basic scheduling, and Elite, aimed at companies that need more advanced project time tracking, real-time project collaboration, and geofencing precision. As of the 2026 pricing structure, Premium costs 20 US dollars per month as a flat base fee plus 10 US dollars per active user per month, while Elite costs 40 US dollars per month as a base fee plus 12 US dollars per active user per month; both figures reflect a price increase implemented on approximately July 1, 2026, which raised the previous per-user rates by roughly 2 US dollars per user across both tiers, a change reported as roughly a 25 percent increase for the Premium tier's per-user fee. Both plans include one free admin license, and a QuickBooks Online subscription is required to use QuickBooks Time, since the two products are designed to work together rather than as fully independent purchases. QuickBooks Time is most commonly compared to standalone time tracking and workforce management tools such as When I Work, Homebase, Deputy, and Toggl Track, and its primary advantage over those competitors is its native, tight integration with QuickBooks Online and QuickBooks Payroll for businesses already using Intuit's accounting ecosystem.

long_form_blocks.heading: Overview
paragraphs: QuickBooks Time is Intuit's employee time tracking and scheduling product, known for most of its history as TSheets. TSheets was founded in 2006 in Eagle, Idaho by CEO Matt Rissell and grew into a significant independent time-tracking company before Intuit acquired it in a deal announced in December 2017 and valued at roughly 340 million US dollars, closing in January 2018.

After the acquisition, the product was gradually rebranded from TSheets by QuickBooks to its current name, QuickBooks Time, and it now operates as a dedicated time tracking and scheduling companion to QuickBooks Online and QuickBooks Payroll, rather than as a standalone accounting product. It remains focused specifically on time tracking, scheduling, and related workforce management rather than broader bookkeeping.

long_form_blocks.heading: Key Features
paragraphs: QuickBooks Time offers GPS-enabled mobile time tracking so field and remote employees can clock in and out from job sites using their phones, along with geofencing that can prompt automatic clock-in and clock-out reminders based on an employee's location. Scheduling tools let managers build and share employee schedules and available shifts, and overtime alerts help prevent unplanned labor cost overruns.

The Elite tier adds more advanced project time tracking and real-time project collaboration features aimed at businesses managing multiple concurrent jobs or projects, while both tiers feed time data directly into QuickBooks Online and QuickBooks Payroll to streamline payroll processing and eliminate manual timesheet entry.

long_form_blocks.heading: Pricing
paragraphs: QuickBooks Time is sold in two tiers, each combining a flat monthly base fee with a per-active-user monthly fee. As of current 2026 pricing, following a rate increase implemented around July 1, 2026, the Premium plan costs 20 US dollars per month base plus 10 US dollars per active user per month, and the Elite plan costs 40 US dollars per month base plus 12 US dollars per active user per month.

Both plans include one free admin license, and a QuickBooks Online subscription is required alongside QuickBooks Time, since the two products are sold and used together rather than fully independently. The July 2026 price increase raised per-user fees by roughly 2 US dollars per user across both tiers compared to prior pricing.

features.title: GPS-enabled mobile time tracking
features.description: Employees clock in and out from their mobile devices with GPS location tagging for field and remote work verification.

features.title: Geofencing reminders
features.description: Automatically prompts employees to clock in or out when they enter or leave a defined job site location.

features.title: Employee scheduling
features.description: Build and share work schedules and shift assignments directly within the platform.

features.title: Overtime alerts
features.description: Notifies managers when employees approach or exceed overtime thresholds to help control labor costs.

features.title: Project time tracking
features.description: Tracks time against specific projects or jobs, available with enhanced detail on the Elite tier.

features.title: Real-time project collaboration
features.description: Elite-tier feature enabling teams to coordinate on project time and status in real time.

features.title: QuickBooks Online and Payroll integration
features.description: Time data flows directly into QuickBooks Online and QuickBooks Payroll for streamlined payroll processing.

features.title: Free admin license
features.description: Both Premium and Elite plans include one complimentary administrator seat.

pros: Native, tight integration with QuickBooks Online and QuickBooks Payroll
GPS and geofencing features well suited to field service and mobile workforces
Scheduling and time tracking combined in a single product
Overtime alerts help control unplanned labor costs
Free admin license included on both pricing tiers
Backed by Intuit's scale and long-term product investment since the 2018 acquisition
cons: Requires an active QuickBooks Online subscription to use, adding to total cost
Per-user pricing increased in July 2026, raising ongoing costs for existing customers
Elite tier's advanced project collaboration features come at a notable price premium
Less useful as a standalone product outside the QuickBooks ecosystem
Base fee plus per-user pricing can add up quickly for larger hourly workforces
Feature parity with dedicated standalone scheduling tools varies by tier

faqs.question: How much does QuickBooks Time cost
faqs.answer: As of 2026, QuickBooks Time Premium costs 20 US dollars per month plus 10 US dollars per active user per month, and Elite costs 40 US dollars per month plus 12 US dollars per active user per month.

faqs.question: What was QuickBooks Time called before
faqs.answer: QuickBooks Time was previously known as TSheets, an independent company founded in 2006 in Eagle, Idaho, which Intuit acquired in a deal that closed in January 2018.

faqs.question: Do I need QuickBooks Online to use QuickBooks Time
faqs.answer: Yes, a QuickBooks Online subscription is required to use QuickBooks Time, since the two products are designed to work together.

faqs.question: What is the difference between QuickBooks Time Premium and Elite
faqs.answer: Premium focuses on attendance tracking and basic scheduling, while Elite adds advanced project time tracking and real-time project collaboration features for a higher price.

faqs.question: Does QuickBooks Time support GPS tracking
faqs.answer: Yes, QuickBooks Time offers GPS-enabled mobile time tracking and geofencing that can prompt automatic clock-in and clock-out reminders based on location.

faqs.question: When did Intuit acquire TSheets
faqs.answer: Intuit announced its acquisition of TSheets on December 5, 2017, in a deal valued at roughly 340 million US dollars, and the acquisition closed on January 11, 2018.

faqs.question: Is there a free admin account with QuickBooks Time
faqs.answer: Yes, both the Premium and Elite plans include one free administrator license.

use_cases.title: Field service and mobile workforce time tracking
use_cases.description: Field service companies use GPS-enabled clock-in and geofencing to accurately track hours for employees working across multiple job sites.
use_cases.audience: Field service, construction, and trade businesses

use_cases.title: Payroll-integrated hourly workforce management
use_cases.description: Businesses already using QuickBooks Online and Payroll use QuickBooks Time to eliminate manual timesheet entry and streamline payroll runs.
use_cases.audience: Small business owners managing hourly employees

use_cases.title: Project-based time and labor cost tracking
use_cases.description: Project-driven businesses use the Elite tier's project time tracking to monitor labor costs and collaboration in real time against specific jobs.
use_cases.audience: Project-based businesses such as contractors and agencies
